title: Zen/photo mgmt
body:
### Placeholder PR for zen/PhotoMgmt

WIP - mobile support for Photo Album and security syncing. This PR will keep zen/PhotoMgmt from being removable.
author: ScottManess

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces mobile sync cursor support for Photo Albums by adding `LinkSyncOrder` / `SecuritySyncOrder` to the `PhotoAlbums` table, updating stored procedures to bump those cursors when links/security change, and documenting the required “sync stamp” contract for future work.

**Changes:**
- Added `LinkSyncOrder` and `SecuritySyncOrder` columns to `PhotoAlbums`, including an upgrade script.
- Updated multiple photo-album stored procedures to bump the appropriate sync cursor after link/security mutations (and before link bulk-delete where required).
- Added AI/domain documentation describing the multi-proc obligations and audit checklist for cursor-style columns.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| docs/ai/index.md | Adds routing entry to the new Photo Albums domain doc. |
| docs/ai/domains/photo-albums.md | New domain doc defining the sync stamp contract and audit checklist. |
| Core/SQL/update_db_AlbumSync.sql | New schema upgrade script adding the two sync cursor columns. |
| Core/SQL/Packer.manifest | Includes the new `update_db_AlbumSync.sql` in the SQL packer manifest. |
| Core/SQL/base_tables.sql | Adds `LinkSyncOrder` / `SecuritySyncOrder` columns to `PhotoAlbums` base schema. |
| Core/SQL/base_procs.sql | Updates photo album/link/security procs to bump sync cursors. |
| Core/SQL/delta_procs.sql | Regenerated procs delta including the new/updated procedures and refresh statements. |
| Core/SQL/record_procs_hash.sql | Updates proc hash to reflect regenerated procs. |
| Core/Common/Components/PhotoManagement/PhotoAlbum.cs | Adds corresponding nullable `DateTimeOffset` properties to the C# model. |
| All.sln | Resolves prior merge conflict markers in solution header. |
| .github/instructions/copilot-sql.instructions.md | Adds guidance for “column migrations with multi-proc obligations,” referencing the worked example. |
</details>
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 16 out of 16 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 15 out of 15 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
reviews.author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer[bot]

review_comments.body: `s_enc_propagate_PhotoAlbumSecurity` now starts an explicit transaction but does not include TRY/CATCH with an explicit ROLLBACK on error. Even with `XACT_ABORT ON`, certain errors can still leave an open transaction and hold locks. Please wrap the transactional block in TRY/CATCH (ROLLBACK if @@TRANCOUNT>0) and COMMIT in the TRY, keeping behavior consistent for new installs vs upgrade scripts.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The temporary upgrade helper proc `dbo.s_util_drop_default` is a write proc but doesn’t set `XACT_ABORT`/`NOCOUNT`, and it builds dynamic SQL by concatenating object names. To make the upgrade script safer and less noisy, add `SET XACT_ABORT ON` + `SET NOCOUNT ON` and use `QUOTENAME()` (or otherwise validate) for `@table_name` / `@default_name` in the `ALTER TABLE ... DROP CONSTRAINT` statement.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `delta_procs.sql` now embeds a developer-specific local path (`C:\Users\smaness\...`) and sqlpackage version metadata. This causes noisy diffs on regeneration and leaks local usernames into the repo. If possible, regenerate via the standard build/CI tooling or adjust the generation step to omit/normalize these header fields so the output is deterministic.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: PR description says this is a placeholder to keep `zen/PhotoMgmt` from being removable, but the change set adds new PhotoAlbums sync-cursor columns + multiple proc updates and documentation. Please update the PR description to reflect the actual scope (mobile album/link/security sync cursor support) so reviewers can assess risk appropriately.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: In `s_enc_get_Albums_added`, `UserAccessType` is computed with `MAX(...)`. Because `PhotoAlbumSecurity` does not enforce uniqueness per (AlbumID, UserID, GroupID), duplicate/dirty rows are possible; with duplicates, `MAX` can return a non-zero access even if a user-level `AccessType = 0` (NoAccess) row exists, which defeats the intended “NoAccess vetoes group/default” rule and can leak albums. Consider computing the effective user access in a way that guarantees 0 wins (e.g., `MIN` for user rows, or an explicit `CASE`/`EXISTS` veto) before applying `COALESCE`.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The new album sync proc hard-codes `OFFSET 0 ROWS` and does not accept an `@Offset` parameter, but the WebAPI and test client expose an `offset` query parameter. Either wire `offset` all the way through (add `@Offset` + use `OFFSET @Offset ROWS`) or remove `offset` from the endpoint/client to avoid misleading pagination behavior.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `s_enc_get_PhotoAlbumLinks_changed` also hard-codes `OFFSET 0 ROWS` and has no `@Offset` parameter, but the API surface includes an `offset` query parameter. This makes `offset` ineffective and can break callers that try to page. Either implement offset in the proc or remove it from the endpoint/client contract.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `offset` is part of this endpoint’s signature and documented, but it is not used (it isn’t passed to `GetAddedAlbumsForMobile`, and the SQL proc currently uses `OFFSET 0 ROWS`). Either remove `offset` from the public API (and test client) or thread it through to the business layer + stored proc so pagination behaves as advertised.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: Same issue here: `offset` is accepted/documented but not used (not passed to the PhotoSystem layer, and the backing SQL proc uses `OFFSET 0 ROWS`). Please either implement offset support end-to-end or remove it from the endpoint/client contract to prevent incorrect pagination assumptions.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: These test client methods send `offset` in the query string, but the new server endpoints currently ignore `offset` (and the SQL procs hard-code `OFFSET 0`). This can hide pagination bugs in tests. Align the client contract with the server (either drop `offset` here or implement offset end-to-end).
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: The new domain doc describes a `LinkSyncOrder` / `SecuritySyncOrder` sync-stamp contract, with `PhotoAlbums` carrying those columns and a multi-proc bump obligation. The actual implementation in this PR does not introduce those columns — `base_tables.sql` still only has `LastModified`, and every new sync proc (`s_enc_get_Albums_added`, `s_enc_get_Albums_removed`, `s_enc_get_PhotoAlbumLinks_changed`) keys off `PhotoAlbums.LastModified` / `PhotoAlbumLinks.LastModified`. A repo-wide search finds `LinkSyncOrder` / `SecuritySyncOrder` only in this doc, `docs/ai/index.md`, and `.github/instructions/copilot-sql.instructions.md` — nowhere in `.sql` or `.cs` source. Either implement the dedicated cursor columns the doc describes, or rewrite the doc (and the `index.md` / copilot-sql instructions references) to describe the actual `LastModified`-based contract this PR ships. As written, the doc and copilot instructions will route future agents to non-existent columns.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `s_enc_get_PhotoAlbumLinks_changed` returns every changed link row in the project regardless of whether the requesting user has access to the parent album. The proc header explicitly documents this as "structural ... access enforcement is the caller's responsibility," but the controller (`GetChangedPhotoLinks` in `SyncController.cs` line 2622) calls only `ConfigureAccessForPortfolioDBUserAsync` and then returns the raw list — no per-album access filter is applied. This leaks the existence of `(AlbumID, PhotoID)` pairs for albums the user has `NoAccess` to (e.g. an admin's private album). The integration test `GetChangedPhotoLinks_NoUserAccessFilter` codifies this leakage as expected behavior. Recommend either filtering links by the same access predicate used in `s_enc_get_Albums_added`, or documenting an explicit security decision that link IDs are non-sensitive — but as currently shipped this contradicts the per-album access filtering applied to `addedAlbums`/`removedAlbums`.
review_comments.author: Copilot

review_comments.body: `s_enc_get_Albums_added` and `s_enc_get_Albums_removed` are write-free read procs but use `OFFSET 0 ROWS` literally instead of accepting an `@Offset` parameter, while every adjacent existing sync proc in this file accepts both `@Limit` and `@Offset` (e.g. `s_enc_get_DMSFolders_added` lines 59777-59778, `s_enc_get_Photos_added` lines 67375-67376, `s_enc_get_Photos_removed` lines 67471-67472). The `APIClientSyncController` helpers and controller endpoints expose an `offset` query string parameter (`/addedAlbums?...&offset=...`, see `APIClientSyncController.cs` lines 719/724/729 and `SyncController.cs` lines 2564/2592/2622), but the value is silently dropped — the business layer `GetAddedAlbumsForMobile`/`GetRemovedAlbumsForMobile`/`GetChangedPhotoLinksForMobile` never pass it to the proc. Either add `@Offset INT` to the three new procs (and thread it through) to match the existing sync proc convention, or remove the unused `offset` parameters from the controller signatures and API client helpers so callers don't pass a value that is silently ignored.
review_comments.author: Copilot

files.patch:
@@ -79,3 +79,17 @@ SET NOCOUNT ON
 ## Scoping
 
 Procedures called from scoped routes (`portfolioGuid`, `projectId`) must filter by that scope. Returning data across all portfolios/projects from a scoped route is a data leakage risk. Intentionally global procedures must use a dedicated unscoped route.
+
+## Column Migrations
+
+When adding a column that must be maintained by multiple stored procedures (e.g., a sync cursor
+that must be bumped whenever a child table changes):
+
+1. `grep base_procs.sql` for every proc that writes to the affected child table.
+2. Verify each proc issues the required bump/update statement.
+3. Add the missing statement to any proc that lacks it.
+
+**Do not stop after updating the first proc found.** This class of column always has multi-proc
+obligations. Declaring done after a single proc is always incomplete.
+
+See `docs/ai/domains/photo-albums.md` for a worked example (`LinkSyncOrder` / `PhotoAlbumLinks`).

+        /// <summary>

+        {
+            await ConfigureAccessForPortfolioDBUserAsync(portfolioGuid, projectId);
+
+            var error = new ErrorEncountered();
+
+            this.RequestedBy.IsMobileUser = true;
+
+            var result = new PhotoSystem().GetPhotoUploaderIdentities(projectId, this.RequestedBy, offset, limit, error);
+            RaiseErrorIfHasMessage(error);
+            RaiseErrorIfTrue(result == null, HttpStatusCode.Forbidden);
+
+            return new ProjectSightActionResult(Ok(result));
+        }

@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+using System;
+
+namespace ProjectSight.Common.Components
+{
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Lightweight album record returned by the mobile album sync endpoint.
+    /// Contains structural data only — no link or security fields.
+    /// </summary>
+    [Serializable]
+    public class PhotoAlbumForMobile
+    {
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Server primary key of the album.
+        /// </summary>
+        public int AlbumID { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Display name of the album.
+        /// </summary>
+        public string Name { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Parent album ID, or null for root-level albums.
+        /// </summary>
+        public int? ParentID { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Timestamp of the last structural change to the album.
+        /// Mobile advances its PhotoAlbumsChanged cursor to this value.
+        /// </summary>
+        public DateTimeOffset LastModified { get; set; }
+
+    }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+using System;
+
+namespace ProjectSight.Common.Components
+{
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Lightweight photo-album link record returned by the mobile changed-photo-links sync endpoint.
+    /// Represents a single link mutation (add or soft-delete) on PhotoAlbumLinks.
+    /// </summary>
+    [Serializable]
+    public class PhotoAlbumLinkForMobile
+    {
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Server primary key of the album.
+        /// </summary>
+        public int AlbumID { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Server primary key of the photo.
+        /// </summary>
+        public int PhotoID { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// True when the link has been soft-deleted (photo removed from album); false when active.
+        /// Mobile applies this as a hard-DELETE on its local PhotoAlbumLinks row.
+        /// </summary>
+        public bool Deleted { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Timestamp of the last change to this link row.
+        /// Mobile advances its PhotoAlbumLinksChanged cursor to this value.
+        /// </summary>
+        public DateTimeOffset LastModified { get; set; }
+    }
+}

@@ -0,0 +1,23 @@
+using System;
+
+namespace ProjectSight.Common.Components
+{
+    /// <summary>
+    /// Tombstone record returned by the mobile removed-albums sync endpoint.
+    /// Contains only the album identity and the timestamp — no name, link, or security fields.
+    /// </summary>
+    [Serializable]
+    public class RemovedAlbumForMobile
+    {
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Server primary key of the deleted album.
+        /// </summary>
+        public int AlbumID { get; set; }
+
+        /// <summary>
+        /// Timestamp at which the album was soft-deleted or security revoked.
+        /// Mobile advances its RemovedAlbums cursor to this value.
+        /// </summary>
+        public DateTimeOffset LastModified { get; set; }
+    }
+}

+/****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_Photo_UploaderIdentity ******/
+CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_enc_get_Photo_UploaderIdentity
+  @ProjectID        int,
+  @Offset           int,
+  @Limit            int
+AS
+  SELECT PhotoID, UploadedByUserID
+    FROM Photos
+   WHERE ProjectID = @ProjectID
+     AND Deleted = 0
+   ORDER BY PhotoID
+  OFFSET @Offset ROWS
+   FETCH NEXT @Limit ROWS ONLY
+GO
+
+/****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_Albums_added ******/
+/* Returns albums added or structurally changed since the given cursor that the requesting user can access.
+   Uses a compound keyset cursor on PhotoAlbums.LastModified + PhotoAlbumID for stable pagination.
+   Candidate albums (project + cursor) are identified first; PhotoAlbumSecurity is then aggregated
+   only for that limited set to avoid a full cross-project scan on large portfolios.
+   Access is evaluated per album: the user-specific AccessType takes priority over the group AccessType
+   (a user-level NoAccess row vetoes group access); when no explicit row exists for the user or group,
+   DefaultAccessType is used as the fallback. Only albums with an effective AccessType > 0 are returned. */
+CREATE PROCEDURE s_enc_get_Albums_added
+  @ProjectID        INT,
+  @UserID           INT,
+  @GroupID          INT,
+  @LastSyncDateTime datetimeoffset,
+  @LastAlbumID      INT,
+  @Limit            INT
+AS
+  /* Identify candidate albums scoped to this project and cursor position first,
+     so the security aggregation below never touches albums outside this window. */
+  WITH candidates AS (
+    SELECT a.PhotoAlbumID,
+           a.AlbumName,
+           a.ParentID,
+           a.LastModified,
+           a.DefaultAccessType
+      FROM PhotoAlbums a
+     WHERE a.ProjectID = @ProjectID
+       AND a.Deleted = 0
+       AND (   a.LastModified > @LastSyncDateTime
+            OR (a.LastModified = @LastSyncDateTime AND a.PhotoAlbumID > @LastAlbumID))
+  ),
+  /* Aggregate security rows in a single pass over PhotoAlbumSecurity, joined to the candidate set.
+     A user-level AccessType = 0 (NoAccess) correctly vetoes any group grant
+     because COALESCE returns 0 before falling through to the group column. */
+  sec AS (
+    SELECT pas.AlbumID,
+           MAX(CASE WHEN pas.UserID = @UserID  AND pas.GroupID IS NULL THEN pas.AccessType END) AS UserAccessType,
+           MAX(CASE WHEN pas.UserID IS NULL AND pas.GroupID = @GroupID THEN pas.AccessType END) AS GroupAccessType
+      FROM PhotoAlbumSecurity pas
+      JOIN candidates c ON c.PhotoAlbumID = pas.AlbumID
+     WHERE (pas.UserID = @UserID  AND pas.GroupID IS NULL)
+        OR (pas.UserID IS NULL AND pas.GroupID = @GroupID)
+     GROUP BY pas.AlbumID
+  )
+  SELECT c.PhotoAlbumID AS AlbumID,
+         c.AlbumName    AS Name,
+         c.ParentID,
+         c.LastModified
+    FROM candidates c
+    LEFT JOIN sec ON sec.AlbumID = c.PhotoAlbumID
+   WHERE COALESCE(sec.UserAccessType, sec.GroupAccessType, c.DefaultAccessType) > 0
+   ORDER BY c.LastModified, c.PhotoAlbumID
+  OFFSET 0 ROWS
+   FETCH NEXT @Limit ROWS ONLY
+GO
+
+/****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_Albums_removed ******/
+/* Returns albums that should be removed from the mobile client since the given cursor.
+   An album qualifies as "removed" when it is soft-deleted (Deleted = 1) OR the requesting
+   user no longer has access (explicit NoAccess, no security row with DefaultAccessType = 0,
+   or group-only access revoked by a user-level override).
+   Uses a compound keyset cursor on PhotoAlbums.LastModified + PhotoAlbumID for stable pagination.
+   Security evaluation mirrors s_enc_get_Albums_added: user-level AccessType overrides group,
+   which overrides DefaultAccessType. Albums with effective access <= 0 are treated as removed. */
+CREATE PROCEDURE s_enc_get_Albums_removed
+  @ProjectID        INT,
+  @UserID           INT,
+  @GroupID          INT,
+  @LastSyncDateTime datetimeoffset,
+  @LastAlbumID      INT,
+  @Limit            INT
+AS
+  /* Identify candidate albums scoped to this project and cursor position first,
+     so the security aggregation below never touches albums outside this window. */
+  WITH candidates AS (
+    SELECT a.PhotoAlbumID,
+           a.LastModified,
+           a.Deleted,
+           a.DefaultAccessType
+      FROM PhotoAlbums a
+     WHERE a.ProjectID = @ProjectID
+       AND (   a.LastModified > @LastSyncDateTime
+            OR (a.LastModified = @LastSyncDateTime AND a.PhotoAlbumID > @LastAlbumID))
+  ),
+  /* Aggregate security rows for the candidate set - same logic as s_enc_get_Albums_added. */
+  sec AS (
+    SELECT pas.AlbumID,
+           MAX(CASE WHEN pas.UserID = @UserID  AND pas.GroupID IS NULL THEN pas.AccessType END) AS UserAccessType,
+           MAX(CASE WHEN pas.UserID IS NULL AND pas.GroupID = @GroupID THEN pas.AccessType END) AS GroupAccessType
+      FROM PhotoAlbumSecurity pas
+      JOIN candidates c ON c.PhotoAlbumID = pas.AlbumID
+     WHERE (pas.UserID = @UserID  AND pas.GroupID IS NULL)
+        OR (pas.UserID IS NULL AND pas.GroupID = @GroupID)
+     GROUP BY pas.AlbumID
+  )
+  SELECT c.PhotoAlbumID AS AlbumID,
+         c.LastModified
+    FROM candidates c
+    LEFT JOIN sec ON sec.AlbumID = c.PhotoAlbumID
+   WHERE c.Deleted = 1
+      OR COALESCE(sec.UserAccessType, sec.GroupAccessType, c.DefaultAccessType) <= 0
+   ORDER BY c.LastModified, c.PhotoAlbumID
+  OFFSET 0 ROWS
+   FETCH NEXT @Limit ROWS ONLY
+GO
+
+/****** Object:  Stored Procedure s_enc_get_PhotoAlbumLinks_changed ******/
+/* Returns PhotoAlbumLinks rows (including soft-deleted) whose LastModified exceeds the compound
+   keyset cursor, scoped to the given project.  Used by the mobile changedPhotoLinks sync stream.
+   No user-access filter is applied — link data is structural; access enforcement is the caller's
+   responsibility.  */
+CREATE PROCEDURE s_enc_get_PhotoAlbumLinks_changed
+  @ProjectID    INT,
+  @LastModified datetimeoffset,
+  @LastAlbumID  INT,
+  @LastPhotoID  INT,
+  @Limit        INT
+AS
+  SELECT pal.AlbumID,
+         pal.PhotoID,
+         pal.Deleted,
+         pal.LastModified
+    FROM PhotoAlbumLinks pal
+   WHERE pal.ProjectID = @ProjectID
+         AND (pal.LastModified > @LastModified 
+             OR (pal.LastModified = @LastModified AND (pal.AlbumID > @LastAlbumID 
+                OR (pal.AlbumID = @LastAlbumID AND pal.PhotoID > @LastPhotoID))))
+   ORDER BY pal.LastModified, pal.AlbumID, pal.PhotoID
+  OFFSET 0 ROWS
+   FETCH NEXT @Limit ROWS ONLY
+GO
+

   @ProjectID        INT ,

@@ -0,0 +1,73 @@
+USE ProjectSight
+GO
+SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON
+GO
+  /* Add a utility proc for dropping default constraints */
+IF OBJECT_ID('dbo.s_util_drop_default') IS NOT NULL
+  DROP PROCEDURE dbo.s_util_drop_default
+GO
+/****** Object: Stored Procedure s_util_drop_default ******/
+CREATE PROCEDURE dbo.s_util_drop_default
+  @table_name     sysname ,
+  @column_name    sysname ,
+  @no_message     bit = 0
+AS
+DECLARE
+  @default_name   sysname ,
+  @table_id       int
+
+  SELECT @default_name = s.name
+    FROM (syscolumns sc INNER JOIN sysobjects s ON sc.cdefault = s.id)
+         INNER JOIN sysobjects pt ON sc.id = pt.id
+   WHERE sc.name = @column_name
+     AND pt.name = @table_name
+
+  IF @default_name IS NOT NULL
+    BEGIN
+      IF @no_message = 0
+        PRINT 'Dropping constraint ' + @default_name + ' from table ' + @table_name
+
+      EXEC('ALTER TABLE dbo.' + @table_name + ' DROP CONSTRAINT ' + @default_name)
+    END
+GO
+
+IF OBJECT_ID('tempdb..#update') IS NOT NULL
+  DROP TABLE #update
+GO
+CREATE TABLE #update
+(
+    update_data          BIT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
+)
+GO
+DELETE FROM #update
+INSERT INTO #update VALUES(0)
+GO
+
+/******************************** BEGINNING OF UPDATES FOR THIS VERSION *************************************/
+
+/* Supporting index for s_enc_get_PhotoAlbumLinks_changed.
+   The proc filters by ProjectID and keysets on (LastModified, AlbumID, PhotoID).
+   Without this index SQL Server scans/sorts the full project's link rows.
+   INCLUDE(Deleted) covers the one non-key column in the SELECT list. */
+IF NOT EXISTS (
+    SELECT 1
+      FROM sys.indexes
+     WHERE object_id = OBJECT_ID('dbo.PhotoAlbumLinks')
+       AND name = 'IX_PhotoAlbumLinks_ProjectID_LastModified_AlbumID_PhotoID'
+)
+BEGIN
+    CREATE NONCLUSTERED INDEX IX_PhotoAlbumLinks_ProjectID_LastModified_AlbumID_PhotoID
+        ON dbo.PhotoAlbumLinks (ProjectID, LastModified, AlbumID, PhotoID)
+        INCLUDE (Deleted)
+END
+GO
+
+/******************************** END OF UPDATES FOR THIS VERSION *************************************/
+
+/*********************Do not make any changes below this section.  -  Start ***************************/
+
+SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER OFF
+GO
+
+DROP PROCEDURE dbo.s_util_drop_default
+GO

@@ -0,0 +1,105 @@
+# Photo Albums Domain
+
+**Source files:**
+- `Core/SQL/base_procs.sql` — `s_enc_add_or_update_PhotoAlbum`, `s_enc_add_or_update_PhotoAlbumLinks`, `s_enc_delete_Photos`, `s_enc_remove_photos_from_album`, `s_enc_propagate_PhotoAlbumSecurity`, `s_enc_add_or_update_PhotoAlbumSecurity`
+- `Core/Business Layer/DataAccess/PhotoManagementSystem.cs`
+- `Core/Common/Components/PhotoManagement/PhotoAlbum.cs`
+- `Core/SQL/base_tables.sql` — `PhotoAlbums`, `PhotoAlbumLinks`, `PhotoAlbumSecurity`
+
+---
+
+## Key Tables
+
+| Table | Purpose |
+|---|---|
+| `PhotoAlbums` | Album record; holds sync cursors for mobile (`LinkSyncOrder`, `SecuritySyncOrder`) |
+| `PhotoAlbumLinks` | Join table — links a `Photo` to an `Album` within a project |
+| `PhotoAlbumSecurity` | Per-user/group access level for an album |
+| `Photos` | Photo record; soft-deleted by `s_enc_delete_Photos` |
+
+---
+
+## Sync Stamp Contract (LinkSyncOrder / SecuritySyncOrder)
+
+`PhotoAlbums` carries two `datetimeoffset NULL` sync cursor columns used by the mobile syncer as high-water marks:
+
+| Column | Bumped when |
+|---|---|
+| `LinkSyncOrder` | Any row in `PhotoAlbumLinks` for this album is inserted, soft-deleted, or restored |
+| `SecuritySyncOrder` | The album's security or visibility changes (to be implemented — see Stories 3+) |
+
+**If a proc mutates `PhotoAlbumLinks` without bumping `LinkSyncOrder`, the mobile client will not re-fetch the changed album.**
+
+### The bump statement
+
+Every proc that inserts, soft-deletes, or restores a `PhotoAlbumLinks` row must issue this UPDATE **after** the DML (see exception below for bulk-delete):
+
+```sql
+/* Bump LinkSyncOrder so the mobile syncer detects this album changed */
+UPDATE PhotoAlbums
+   SET LinkSyncOrder        = @WhenRequested
+ WHERE PhotoAlbumID = @AlbumID   -- use the relevant album ID variable
+```
+
+### Exception — bump before soft-delete when album ID comes from a subquery
+
+When the affected album IDs must be discovered via a subquery of `PhotoAlbumLinks` (e.g. `s_enc_delete_Photos`, which has no `@AlbumID` parameter), bump **before** the soft-delete so the subquery still finds the un-deleted rows:
+
+```sql
+/* Bump LinkSyncOrder on all albums that contain this photo BEFORE soft-deleting their links */
+UPDATE PhotoAlbums
+   SET LinkSyncOrder        = @WhenRequested
+ WHERE PhotoAlbumID IN (SELECT AlbumID FROM PhotoAlbumLinks WHERE PhotoID = @PhotoID AND Deleted = 0)
+
+UPDATE PhotoAlbumLinks
+   SET Deleted = 1, ...
+ WHERE PhotoID = @PhotoID AND Deleted = 0
+```
+
+### Procs with this obligation
+
+| Proc | Operation on PhotoAlbumLinks | Bump target | Order |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| `s_enc_add_or_update_PhotoAlbumLinks` | INSERT new link | `@AlbumID` | After INSERT |
+| `s_enc_add_or_update_PhotoAlbumLinks` | Soft-delete source link on move | `@OldAlbumID` | After UPDATE |
+| `s_enc_add_or_update_PhotoAlbumLinks` | Restore soft-deleted link | `@AlbumID` | After UPDATE |
+| `s_enc_delete_Photos` | Bulk soft-delete all links for the photo | Subquery on `PhotoAlbumLinks` | **Before** UPDATE |
+| `s_enc_remove_photos_from_album` | Soft-delete specific link | `@AlbumID` | After UPDATE |
+| `s_enc_add_or_update_PhotoAlbum` | INSERT the album itself | `LinkSyncOrder` in INSERT column list | Inline in INSERT |
+
+### Adding a new proc that writes to PhotoAlbumLinks
+
+1. **INSERT** — add the bump after the INSERT.
+2. **Soft-delete (UPDATE Deleted=1)** — if album ID is available as a variable, bump after; if it must be found via a subquery on `PhotoAlbumLinks`, bump **before**.
+3. **Restore (UPDATE Deleted=0)** — bump after the restore UPDATE.
+
+---
+
+## Move-to-root is a no-op
+
+`s_enc_add_or_update_PhotoAlbumLinks` does a `RETURN` when a photo is moved to the root album (the album with `ParentID IS NULL`). No rows are written to `PhotoAlbumLinks`, so no `LinkSyncOrder` bump is needed on that path.
+
+---
+
+## Common Mistakes
+
+| Mistake | Consequence |
+|---|---|
+| Adding `LinkSyncOrder` to the `INSERT INTO PhotoAlbums` column list without auditing `PhotoAlbumLinks`-writing procs | Mobile clients don't see changes made by those procs |
+| Bumping `LinkSyncOrder` after soft-deleting links when the album ID comes from a subquery | The subquery finds zero rows; bump is a no-op |
+| Using `@AlbumID` in the bump on the move-source path instead of `@OldAlbumID` | Source album goes un-bumped; mobile doesn't detect the removal |
+
+---
+
+## Audit checklist — adding a new sync cursor column to PhotoAlbums
+
+When adding any column to `PhotoAlbums` that must be maintained by multiple procs:
+
+1. Add the column to `base_tables.sql`.
+2. Add the delta `ALTER TABLE` to `update_db_{FeatureName}.sql`.
+3. Add the property to `PhotoAlbum.cs`.
+4. `grep base_procs.sql` for every proc that writes to the relevant child table (`PhotoAlbumLinks`, `PhotoAlbumSecurity`, etc.).
+5. For each proc found, verify the bump statement is present and correctly ordered.
+6. Add the bump to any proc missing it.
+
+Do not stop after updating the first proc. Steps 4–6 are mandatory.
